mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns are positive in >60% of observations where UNRATE > 5.0, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer demand and economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20d returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with rising INDPRO during easing periods, or the signal reverses direction in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 195.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces monetary tightening and erodes real corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60d returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening manufacturing demand and impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >60% of months with falling INDPRO during late-cycle phases, or the regime-specific signal fails to outperform a baseline model.
